1.matchText: the previous book in this chain spent twenty chapters describing
1.blurb: The Scientific Revolution is the book this one is handed from. Over twenty chapters it traces how Copernicus, Kepler, Galileo, Bacon, Descartes, Boyle, and finally Newton built a method for producing knowledge that did not depend on authority. Its last chapter asks why that machine locked into place in Europe, and explicitly hands the new science forward to the generation that would turn it into a theory of politics and progress.

3.matchText: Song China had built the first true mass-print culture seven centuries earlier
3.blurb: The Northern Song ran what that narrative calls the first true mass-publishing culture in human history: cheap woodblock-printed books, a huge examination-driven literate class, and intellectual debate conducted in print at something like the volume Europe would reach only in the eighteenth century. The decisive difference was political. Song print culture sat inside one unified empire that could, in principle, control it; Europe's sat across a patchwork of rival states, so a book banned in Paris was simply printed in Amsterdam.

4.matchText: the algebra developed in India and the medieval Islamic world
4.blurb: The mathematics Newton's system rested on was not a European invention. The decimal place-value numerals came from India, and algebra was developed and named in Abbasid Baghdad, alongside the precise astronomical tables later astronomers built on. The Enlightenment preached Newton as if light had switched on in one English orchard; the raw material had been assembled, over centuries, somewhere else.

6.matchText: Ottoman envoys were sending home their own bewildered letters about Paris
6.blurb: While Montesquieu invented a fictional Persian writing home in bafflement about France, the real traffic ran the other way too. During the Ottoman Tulip Era, envoys like Yirmisekiz Çelebi were sent to Paris and wrote widely-read embassy memoirs describing French customs with exactly the estranging wonder Montesquieu's device imitated, and the era's cautious Europeanizing was ended by a revolt in 1730 in the same pattern of top-down reform broken by entrenched interests the Enlightenment would watch repeatedly.

9.matchText: The Song polymath Shen Kuo had read the same story in fossil seashells
9.blurb: Around 1088 the Northern Song official Shen Kuo found marine fossils stranded in mountain strata and concluded the land had once been seabed and the world was vastly older than tradition allowed, seven centuries before Hutton. The inference was not the European novelty. What was new in Scotland was the systematic argument from processes still visibly at work, and an intellectual culture that pressed the conclusion against scripture in public.

10.matchText: ran the rich Indian province of Bengal as a private extraction operation
10.blurb: The other side of Smith's attack on the chartered companies is told from the receiving end. After Plassey in 1757 the East India Company became the tax-collecting power in Bengal, and its plunder culminated in the catastrophic Bengal famine of 1770. The wealth that helped industrialize Britain and bankroll its politics was, by a chain the salons did not trace, extracted there.

11.matchText: The Mughal emperor Akbar abolished the
11.blurb: Religious toleration was argued and practiced as state policy long before Locke wrote it down. The Mughal emperor Akbar abolished the jizya, the tax on non-Muslims, in 1564 and from 1575 hosted a hall of inter-faith debate at his court, building a Hindu-Muslim joint sovereignty more than a century before the European toleration tracts. The Enlightenment largely forgot to credit it.

11.matchText: the ancient atomists Lucretius had transmitted
11.blurb: The materialist idea that the universe is atoms moving in void, with no intervening personal god, was argued by the Greek atomists Democritus and Leucippus in the fifth century BCE and transmitted to Rome in the poem of Lucretius. The Enlightenment's boldest atheists were, knowingly, reviving a two-thousand-year-old position, not inventing one.

12.matchText: Patronizing Voltaire was cheaper than freeing a serf, and it bought a better press
12.blurb: For the full picture of how the Nakaz was written and shelved, follow the thread forward into the Russian Empire narrative. Its chapter on Catherine traces the Legislative Commission from grand convocation to quiet dissolution, the gap between the borrowed Montesquieu rhetoric and the absence of any new code, and exactly where the limits of enlightened absolutism bit. It is the inside view of the regime the salons were applauding.

12.matchText: After a massive Cossack-and-peasant rising (the Pugachev rebellion of 1773
12.blurb: The Russian Empire narrative gives the Pugachev revolt a full chapter — the scale of a rising that raised tens of thousands and burned across the Volga, and the paradox the parenthetical here cannot hold: the revolt frightened Catherine into tightening serfdom rather than loosening it, and produced the 1785 Charter to the Nobility as its institutional result. The failure of enlightened reform in Russia is not an absence of will but a documented reversal under pressure.

12.matchText: abolished serfdom in the Habsburg lands
12.blurb: Joseph II made the choice Catherine refused. The Russian Empire narrative shows the same decision point from inside the Russian court: Catherine weighing precisely the options Joseph would take, and choosing the press of Paris over the freeing of a serf. Read alongside this chapter, it gives the inside view of why the two enlightened absolutists, facing the same question, diverged so completely.

15.matchText: every authority since antiquity had said was impossible
15.blurb: The authority since antiquity is classical Athens. Its direct democracy under Cleisthenes and Pericles was the model every later theorist measured a republic against, and the reason republics were thought to die at scale. It is also where the Enlightenment's deepest contradiction had already appeared once: a citizen democracy funded by empire and sustained by slavery, exactly the structure the new American republic would reproduce.

16.matchText: building Britain's Indian empire even as Britain lost its American one
16.blurb: The same global war that bankrupted the French monarchy and triggered 1789 was, on the other side of the world, the war in which Britain lost thirteen American colonies and consolidated its Indian empire through the East India Company. The rights-of-man decade was also the decade of the largest European imperial acquisition; the two halves of the contradiction Chapter 18 detonates were running at once.
